column1: DVA-C02,"Domain 1: Development with AWS Services","Development with AWS Services","## DVA-C02 Domain 1: Development with AWS Services
This domain covers Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB, S3, SQS/SNS, Kinesis, Step Functions, and SDK patterns.

column3: API Gateway, ALB, Cognito, Step Functions

column4: Retries 2x; DLQ or Destinations for failures
Lambda polls; batch processing; retry until expiry

column3: 50 MB zipped / 250 MB unzipped (or 10 GB container)

column1: COLD START: Download code -> Start runtime -> Initialize (outside handler)
WARM INVOCATION: Execute handler only (SDK clients already initialized)
- Initialize SDK clients OUTSIDE handler (reused across invocations)

column3: Guarantees AND caps at N concurrent

column1: **Throttling:** Sync = 429 error. Async = retries then DLQ. Event Source = retries until expiry.
- **ReportBatchItemFailures:** Only retry failed items (partial batch response)
Use: API Gateway points to alias; CodeDeploy shifts traffic

column3: Pass entire request to Lambda (most common)

column1: - **Usage Plans + API Keys:** Rate limit third-party consumers
- **CORS:** Lambda must return headers + enable OPTIONS method
- **Sort Key (SK):** Optional; enables range queries within partition
- **Capacity:** On-Demand (pay per request) or Provisioned (set RCU/WCU)
- **Consistency:** Eventually consistent (default, 2x throughput) or Strongly consistent
RCU: 1 strongly consistent read/sec for item <= 4 KB
2 eventually consistent reads/sec for item <= 4 KB
Formula: RCU = (reads/sec) x CEIL(item_size / 4 KB) / consistency_factor
**Important:** FilterExpression does NOT reduce capacity - filters AFTER reading.
**GSI Throttling:** If GSI WCU exhausted, base table writes throttled too.
- **Use cases:** Trigger Lambda on changes, cross-region replication, analytics
- Automatically deletes expired items (epoch timestamp attribute)
- Free (no WCU consumed); deletion within 48 hours
- **Pre-signed URL (GET):** Temporary download of private object
- **Pre-signed URL (PUT):** Allow upload without AWS credentials
- **Event Notifications:** Trigger Lambda/SQS/SNS on object events
- **EventBridge:** Advanced filtering on S3 events (metadata, size, tags)
Publisher -> SNS Topic -> SQS Queue A (service 1)
- **Message Filtering:** Subscribers receive only matching messages (filter policy on attributes)
- **FIFO Topics:** Ordered delivery to FIFO SQS queues
- **Exponential backoff with jitter:** wait = random(0, min(2^attempt, 30))
- **Waiters:** Poll until resource reaches desired state
**Shard capacity:** 1 MB/s write, 2 MB/s read per shard.
- ""Process SQS; one fails, batch retries"" -> ReportBatchItemFailures
- ""Can't access internet from VPC"" -> NAT Gateway or VPC endpoints
- ""CORS errors"" -> Lambda returns CORS headers + OPTIONS method
- ""Rate limit consumers"" -> Usage Plans + API Keys
This covers all objectives in DVA-C02 Domain 1:
- **Lambda** - Invocation models, lifecycle, concurrency, versions/aliases, layers, destinations
- **API Gateway** - REST/HTTP/WebSocket, proxy integration, stages, caching, throttling, CORS
- **DynamoDB** - Keys, capacity, Query vs. Scan, indexes, streams, conditional writes, TTL
- **S3** - Pre-signed URLs, multipart upload, event notifications, storage classes
- **SQS** - Standard vs. FIFO, visibility timeout, DLQ, long polling, partial batch
- **Kinesis** - Streams vs. SQS, shards, partition keys, consumers
- **Step Functions** - State types, Standard vs. Express, retry/catch
- **SDK Patterns** - Retry, exponential backoff, pagination, waiters

column2: +-- Invocation: Synchronous, Asynchronous, Event Source Mapping
+-- Lifecycle: Cold start (init) -> Warm invocation (handler only)
+-- Concurrency: Unreserved, Reserved (cap), Provisioned (no cold start)
+-- Versions & Aliases: Immutable versions; weighted aliases for deployment
+-- Layers: Shared libraries (up to 5, 250 MB total unzipped)
+-- Destinations: Success/failure routing (SQS, SNS, Lambda, EventBridge)
+-- Best Practices: Init outside handler; minimize package; use layers
+-- Integration: Lambda Proxy (common), Custom, AWS Service, Mock
+-- Stages: dev/staging/prod; stage variables for config
+-- Throttling: Account (10K) -> Stage -> Method -> Usage Plan
+-- CORS: Lambda returns headers (proxy); OPTIONS method (preflight)
+-- Usage Plans + API Keys: Rate limiting third-party consumers
+-- Keys: Partition Key (hash) + optional Sort Key (range)
+-- Reads: Eventually Consistent (2x) vs. Strongly Consistent
+-- Indexes: GSI (any key, eventually consistent, own capacity)

column3: LSI (same PK, different SK, strongly consistent)

column2: +-- Operations: Query (efficient, by PK) vs. Scan (expensive, full table)
+-- Streams: CDC (24h retention); trigger Lambda on changes
+-- Expressions: Conditional writes, update expressions, filters
+-- TTL: Auto-delete expired items (free, no WCU consumed)
+-- Patterns: Optimistic locking, atomic counters, large objects->S3
+-- Pre-signed URLs: Temporary access without credentials
+-- Multipart Upload: Required > 5 GB; recommended > 100 MB
+-- Event Notifications: Lambda, SQS, SNS, EventBridge
+-- Storage Classes: Standard -> IA -> Glacier (lifecycle rules)
+-- Versioning: Keep all versions; MFA Delete for protection
+-- Transfer Acceleration: CloudFront edge for fast upload
+-- Standard: Unlimited throughput, at-least-once, best-effort ordering
+-- FIFO: 300-3000 msg/s, exactly-once, strict ordering per group
+-- Dead Letter Queue: Capture failed messages (maxReceiveCount)
+-- Extended Client: Large messages via S3 (> 256 KB)
+-- Lambda: Batch processing, partial batch failures
+-- Pub/Sub: Push to multiple subscribers simultaneously
+-- Protocols: Lambda, SQS, HTTP/S, Email, SMS, Firehose, Mobile
+-- Fan-out: SNS -> multiple SQS queues (decouple consumers)
+-- FIFO Topics: Ordered delivery to FIFO SQS queues
+-- Data Streams: Real-time ingestion (shards, partition keys)
+-- Consumers: Shared (2 MB/s/shard) vs. Enhanced Fan-Out (per consumer)
+-- Ordering: Per shard (same partition key -> same shard)
+-- vs. SQS: Multiple consumers, replay, real-time analytics
+-- States: Task, Choice, Wait, Parallel, Map, Pass, Succeed, Fail
+-- Workflows: Standard (1 year, exactly-once) vs. Express (5 min, high volume)
+-- Map State: Process arrays (inline or distributed)
+-- Callback Pattern: Wait for external completion (task token)
+-- GraphQL: Managed API with schema-first design
+-- Data Sources: DynamoDB, Lambda, RDS, HTTP, OpenSearch
+-- Auth: API Key, Cognito, IAM, OIDC, Lambda

column1: +-- Waiters: Poll until resource reaches desired state
+-- Error Handling: ClientError, throttling, service exceptions
+-- Credentials: Environment vars -> IAM role -> config file (chain)
- **Lambda init code** (outside handler) runs once per cold start, reused across warm invocations. #1 best practice.
- **Lambda Proxy Integration** requires statusCode, headers, body in response. Wrong format = 502.
- **DynamoDB FilterExpression** does NOT reduce RCU. Filters AFTER reading. Use Query + proper keys instead.
- **GSI throttling** propagates to base table. Ensure GSI WCU >= base table WCU.
- **SQS Visibility Timeout** must be > Lambda timeout. Otherwise messages reappear (duplicates).
- **Long Polling** (WaitTimeSeconds: 1-20) reduces costs. Always enable unless immediate response needed.
- **Pre-signed URLs** inherit creator's permissions. Revoked permissions = URL stops working.
- **SNS + SQS fan-out** = standard pattern for one event to multiple consumers.
- **Kinesis vs. SQS:** Multiple consumers + replay = Kinesis. Simple decoupling = SQS.
- **Step Functions:** Standard = long-running, exactly-once. Express = high-volume, short, cheaper.
- **HTTP API vs. REST API:** HTTP is 70% cheaper and faster. REST for caching, WAF, usage plans.
- **DynamoDB Streams + Lambda** = event-driven pattern for table changes.
- **FilterCriteria** on event source mapping reduces invocations (filter at source, not in code).
- **ReportBatchItemFailures** prevents entire batch retries on single failure.
- **S3 -> EventBridge** preferred over direct notifications for advanced filtering.
